bottom_line: Eliminate the recurring make test-unit ModuleNotFoundError: anthropic gate break by making that dependency reliably available (or test-import-guarded) in the standard project test environment.
coverage_notes: DB telemetry was available (iw db-identity check: UP; item-status JSON reviewed). Reviewed all per-step run logs directly except large test-output logs (S11/S12), which were sampled via grep for failure/error patterns. Reviewed S05 fix-cycle logs and final reports for retry context.

findings.title: Repeated S05 gate failure on missing anthropic dependency
findings.recommendation: Ensure the test environment always provides anthropic (or guard that test module import so unrelated items are not blocked by optional-provider availability).
evidence: ai-dev/logs/I-00115_S05_run1.log:9 — make test-unit ❌ (ModuleNotFoundError: anthropic in unit test collection)
ai-dev/logs/I-00115_S05_run3.log:8 — make test-unit ❌ (ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'anthropic' during collection)
ai-dev/logs/I-00115_S05_run5.log:13 — make test-unit ❌ failed (ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'anthropic')
findings.paste_prompt: /iw-new-cr Stabilize test environment by ensuring anthropic is available (or import-guarded) so S05 make test-unit no longer fails on unrelated items; context: I-00115 self-assess report at ai-dev/active/I-00115/reports/I-00115_self_assess_report.md.
